mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending, which forces S&P 500 earnings down and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand growth, which lifts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates supply chain bottlenecks and margin compression, triggering negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.0 percent increases discount rates and reduces equity present values, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS stays above 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening, which boosts wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The divergent movement of PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to yield a positive Rank IC greater than 0.03 for 60-day forward returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts signal rising household wealth and construction sector activity, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: HOUST increases are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of monthly observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index above 90.0 indicates robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in over 60% of instances where INDPRO exceeds the 90.0 threshold during recovery phases.
